ai_overview: NOTE: Viktor's Stakeholder Digest email was not retrieved — Microsoft 365 authentication is pending. This digest is built from Slack and Jira sources only for Tuesday 29 April 2026. It was a productive mid-sprint day led by the design team. Ciara Carroll published a comprehensive Claude Design team briefing across #gdc-pod and #core-product, establishing org-wide etiquette and workflows for using Claude Design vs Figma — one of the most substantive enablement posts of the quarter. Jessica Dordevic was active on IUJ Banners (GDCU-8928), coordinating Figma branch cleanup and DE HPC article UX. On the Jira front: Sanja Budic progressed the Betting Tips Hub (GDCU-8907) and a US Prediction Market sitemap fix (GDCU-8895); Kelvin Yee has two hotfix tickets active; Carlos Blauth completed the Free to Play App POC v2 (GDCU-8871); and Nebojsa Stanic closed out a page performance investigation (GDCU-8870). Senior stakeholders were engaged: Michael Butlin gave detailed Learning Hub imagery feedback in a group DM, and Dan Smullen led an SEO thread on surviving Google's zero-click era in #seochatter. Vic Dadson remains on medical leave; Milan Nikolic is on annual leave.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: No activity on 29 April. Remains on medical sick leave signed off by her doctor on 21 April. No Jira or Slack activity detected. She had messaged Mike directly about her condition and a family bereavement prior to leave; HR and Bob have been notified.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: Highly active on 29 April across Slack and Jira. In #gdc-product-design-pod she shared the DE HPC mobile review Figma file, discussed HTML-for-editors changes with Ciara, and contributed to Figma branch cleanup (16 open branches reviewed for merge). In a DM with Mike she shared the IUJ Banners Figma (GDCU-8928) and requested text sign-off. Jira: 5 tickets active — GDCU-8928 (IUJ Banners, In Progress), GDCU-8879 (Genesis Brand Carousel, In Progress), GDCU-8546 (Tinder Style Offer Match, In Progress), GDCU-8509 (Genesis Dashboard Home, To Do), GDCU-8325 (Branded Items for Infogram, Backlog).

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: Active on 29 April via DMs with Mike on Claude Design prototype progress — confirmed remaining minor changes will be done in Figma rather than Claude Design before closing off. Jira: GDCU-8813 (Ace Navigation Assistant UI Audit, In Product) and GDCU-8904 (Group Bets Widget, In Progress) both updated. Earlier this week in #gdc-product-led-growth he shared his Claude Design workflow: fast iterative prototyping in Claude before finalising in Figma, saving several hours per component.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: Very active on 29 April. Published a major Claude Design team briefing (1,400+ words) simultaneously to #gdc-pod and #core-product covering what Claude Design is, what GDC has built into the design system (3,000+ components, brand identity, Orion Git integration), team etiquette, and usage guidelines for creatives vs non-creatives — a significant org enablement action. In #gdc-product-design-pod she initiated Figma branch cleanup ahead of today's release and raised a pros-and-cons dev ticket discussion. Also posted CA/FR oplist card designs to #gdc-product-led-growth on 28 April. Consulted by Mike on the betslip Figma file access and green-light status (per Dean's DM).

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: Light activity on 29 April. In #gdc-product-design-pod he raised a sensible planning question — proposing to close the Slot Hub Search Refinements ticket (GDCU-8737) given the broader Search & Filter Components Audit (GDCU-8685) is planned for next sprint. Previously posted the full Delivery Cycle 7 Week 1 & 2 release summary to #gdc-core (17 April) and managed DC8 high-priority planning. Monitoring ticket queue and making pragmatic scope decisions.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Active across multiple channels on 29 April. In #gdc-pod she sent the sprint retro reminder and checked on Learning Hub QA status. In #gdc-product-design-pod she raised a dev ticket for the pros-and-cons component update and queried closing the design system ticket (GDCU-8893). In #gdc-nonen-sync she flagged two live content issues: snippets breaking the NL casino strategy page navigation, and brown text on a Norwegian slots page — both requiring editorial fixes. Also shared the mid-week catchup agenda on 28 April.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: Jira shows GDCU-8871 (🔥 Free to Play App POC v2) moved to Done on 29 April — a significant milestone for the FTP App workstream. No Slack activity detected on 29 April. No further assigned open Jira tickets visible in the current sprint snapshot.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: No observable Jira or Slack activity on 29 April. Most recent known contribution was 22 April in a DM with Mike, providing a technical assessment on Betting Tips Hub pagination load — confirming ~30 tips per load should be manageable via the Matrix API. Created and manages the #gdc-high-agency channel. No Jira tickets updated.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: Active in Jira on 29 April with two tickets in progress: GDCU-8842 (Integrate Genesis Content Layouts — a DC8 high-priority item, updated this morning 30 Apr 08:29) and GDCU-8834 (Implement Fixes for Cool Tones Update, updated 29 Apr 18:31). Gabriel Cornoiu consulted him directly in #6amigos on 29 April about whether Tailwind CSS grid classes could be the solution for the content layouts integration, suggesting active technical discussion.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: Two hotfix tickets active in Jira on 29 April: GDCU-8900 (🔥 SlotsLaunch Game Grid Shortcode, moved to In QA on 29 Apr 14:40) and GDCU-8878 (🔥 Implement Oplist Redesign on Online Casinos Page CA/FR, In Progress, updated 29 Apr 12:13). No Slack activity detected. The CA/FR oplist redesign is a DC8 high-priority delivery.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No Jira or Slack activity on 29 April. Was noted as on Annual Leave in the PDMT channel on 13 April, with Gabriel flagging spare capacity in the upcoming sprint as a result. Last known Slack message was March 2026 regarding a 5pm meeting time conflict for Serbian timezone.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: Jira shows GDCU-8870 (Investigate Performance Improvements for Page Timeouts) moved to Done on 29 April 12:35 — completing a technical investigation ticket. No Slack activity detected on 29 April. This was his only ticket visible in the recent Jira sprint update.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: No Jira or Slack activity detected on 29 April. Was sick on 27 April (messaged #gdc-pod: 'I am sick as well as my kid so I will be taking a day off'). On 23 April Shane confirmed Novica was working on mobile bottom nav bumps post-release. Current work status for 29 April is unclear.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: Active in Jira on 29 April with two tickets: GDCU-8907 (🔥 Implement the Betting Tips Hub Page [GX], In Progress, updated 30 Apr 08:07) and GDCU-8895 (Prediction Market Page Not Appearing on US Sitemap, In QA, updated 29 Apr 16:40). The Betting Tips Hub is a DC8 hotfix priority for the GX geo. No Slack activity on 29 April — last Slack contribution was the deep-link API performance flag in early April.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: Light activity on 29 April. Posted a congratulations message in #peoplenews (29 Apr 16:40). Jira shows GDCU-8867 (GDC Regression Testing GDCU Sprint 8) remains In QA, updated 29 Apr 11:27 — active sprint regression work underway. On 28 April asked in #gdc-pod about who receives the ChatGPT verification code at the team email address.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: No Jira or Slack activity detected on 29 April. Last Slack activity was 24 April asking in #gdc-pod about Bloomreach access. Most substantive recent work was Prism CMS feedback in late March (detailed QA bug reports: duplicate taxonomy 500 error, image rename not reflecting on frontend, change sets UI issues). No active Jira tickets visible in current sprint.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: Active in DMs on 29 April. In a DM with Mike (09:30) he asked whether Ciara had been given a green light to proceed with betslip design files — confirming she still needed access/approval from Cameron. Budget discussion also noted (11:17-11:20). In a group DM with Gabriel, Owen and Svetlana (09:27, 10:15) had brief coordination exchanges. Engaged on product decisions but no major strategic outputs on 29 April.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: Meaningfully active on 29 April via DMs with Mike and a group DM with Mike and Ciara. Provided detailed review feedback on Learning Hub card imagery (09:24-13:00): flagged inconsistency between generic stock-like desktop images and red/neon-style mobile images, identified specific category mismatches (Core Principles, Blackjack Strategy Trainer icons), and recommended a focused approach using the mobile red/neon style for tomorrow's release while planning image improvements subsequently. Active and engaged reviewer on learning hub pre-launch quality.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: Active in #seochatter on 29 April (10:01-10:24), leading a substantive thread on '17 Content Types to Survive Google's Zero-Click Future'. Shared analysis from Cyrus Shepard noting ~60% of Google searches now end without a click, with AI Mode set to push this higher. Outlined three survival content types: Owned Audience channels, Transaction Pages enabling user actions, and Original Research/events — with examples relevant to the GDC group portfolio. Balanced with a note that commercial-intent queries are what the group cares about most.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: Light activity on 29 April. In #6amigos (15:55) consulted Harden Wallace directly about whether Tailwind CSS grid classes could solve the 'content layouts' integration for GDCU-8842. In #gdc-pod (09:39) reminded the team that the Learning Hub goes live with the weekly release. Earlier in the week (20 April in #gdc-pdmt) announced repurposing the PDMT meeting — now used for planning every 3 weeks, with off-weeks serving as a team weekly.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: No Jira or Slack activity detected on 29 April. Last significant activity was 16 April in #core-division announcing the Iceland geo launch and thanking the cross-team effort. Most recent DM with Mike was 22 April when Mike shared the Clarity API key for analytics access.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: No Slack or Jira activity on 29 April specifically. Was active on 27-28 April in #core-division-leads: sent a detailed Q1 earnings call prep request asking leads to submit wins by category (AI Transformation, Channel Diversification, GEO Growth, SEO, Product & Campaigns), and reminded the team of a finance P&L review due Thursday COB. Previously flagged NZ regulatory concerns (potential affiliate marketing ban) and discussed the revenue reporting consolidation to the Finance report as the single source of truth.
